meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS MVP.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, use Claude Code to rapidly scaffold and deploy functional SaaS micro-tools. By integrating Antigravity for architectural planning, you transform abstract ideas into production-grade code in hours, creating concrete proof of your engineering velocity and product-market fit for potential investors and early adopters.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen, the silence of your repository echoing the stagnation of your venture. The market doesn't care about your potential; it cares about your shipping velocity. Without a portfolio, you are invisible, a ghost in the machine of the SaaS ecosystem. 

But the era of the 'solo developer' has been replaced by the era of the 'AI Architect.' By mastering Claude Code and Antigravity, you stop writing boilerplate and start orchestrating sophisticated, revenue-generating software. This guide is your bridge from 'no portfolio' to 'shippable product' in a single weekend.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, define a high-value micro-SaaS feature. Focus on a single problem that provides instant utility, ensuring your portfolio piece demonstrates both technical skill and business acumen.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a 3-day MVP scope for a [Insert Idea] that solves [Insert Problem]. Focus on core functionality that demonstrates database integration, API handling, and clean UI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this MVP scope and suggest a lean folder structure for a Next.js/Supabase stack.'

steps.title: Architectural Foundation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the project architecture. A professional folder structure is the first thing a recruiter or investor looks for to judge your seniority.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready folder structure for a SaaS MVP. Include patterns for modular components, API routes, and environment configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the directory structure and initialize the project with the specified stack.'

steps.title: Rapid Logic Implementation
steps.description: Implement core business logic using Claude Code. Focus on clean, readable code that handles edge cases—this is what separates a junior script from a professional asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure, scalable function for [Specific Feature] that adheres to SOLID principles and includes error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core logic for [Feature] and write unit tests for the primary success path.'

steps.title: Polishing the UX
steps.description: A portfolio piece must look premium. Use Claude Code to refine your UI components, ensuring a consistent design language that implies a high-end product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current UI component and suggest 3 improvements for better accessibility and conversion-focused design.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the suggested UI improvements and ensure mobile responsiveness.'

steps.title: Documentation & Deployment
steps.description: A repository without a README is just a folder of files. Document your architectural decisions to show you think like a Senior Engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for this project that highlights the problem, the tech stack, and the business value delivered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a README.md and verify all environment variables are documented.'

pro_tips: Treat your GitHub commit history as a product log—commit often with descriptive, professional messages.
Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README to showcase your problem-solving process.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation for your API endpoints immediately after building them.
Prioritize building one high-complexity feature over five simple 'Todo App' clones.
Always deploy your MVP to a live URL; a working link is worth ten times the value of a local repo.
content.next_steps: Once your first MVP is live, link the repository to your LinkedIn profile and share the project on X or IndieHackers to get your first real-world feedback.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code brings you closer to your first paying customer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
